summary.notes: 1 FAIL (p072::2 Protocol definition wrong concept). 7 WARN including: p074::8 narrow law definition, p075::0 imprecise renewal rationale, p076 OSS ZH punctuation+terminology inconsistency, p078 bullet marker mismatch, p079::1 informal register, p079::2 oversimplified access def, p081::1 label-not-definition.

findings.detail: Protocol (プロトコル) is a communication rule/standard between systems (e.g. TCP/IP, HTTP), NOT 'conventions when using a programming language'. JP def '프로그램言語を使うときの特別な約束', ZH '使用程序设计语言时的特殊约定', EN 'Special conventions used when working with a programming language' all state the wrong scope. Must read: communication protocol = agreed rules for data exchange between network participants.

fix_suggestion.jp: コンピュータ同士がデータをやり取りするときのルール（通信規約）
fix_suggestion.zh: 计算机之间进行数据交换时遵循的规则（通信协议）
fix_suggestion.en: A set of rules governing how computers exchange data (communication standard)

findings.term_jp: 著作権と産業財産権の違い (table header: 主な対象者)
findings.detail: Header cell '主な対象者' rendered as 'Main Target' (EN). In a rights context, 'Primary Rights Holder' or 'Main Subject/Holder' reads more naturally. 'Target' can imply adversarial connotation in English legal contexts.

Unfair Competition Prevention Act (不正競争防止法) protects more than just 営業秘密 (trade secrets); it also covers look-alike goods, false representations, domain name misappropriation, etc. All three language versions frame it solely as 'protecting trade secrets / preventing rival-company unfair competition', which is too narrow for exam coverage. Japan-jurisdiction: Act is domestic Japanese law (平成5年法律第47号).

fix_suggestion.jp: 不正な競争行為（営業秘密の侵害・商品形態の模倣・虚偽表示など）を規制する法律
fix_suggestion.zh: 规制不正当竞争行为（侵害营业秘密、仿冒商品形态、虚假表示等）的法律
fix_suggestion.en: A law regulating unfair competitive acts including trade secret infringement, product imitation, and false representations (Japan: Act No. 47 of 1993)

findings.detail: Table p074::9 rows contain full definitions identical to standalone term entities p074::0-3 on the same page. No content inconsistency detected between table rows and standalone terms; duplication is structural/by design. Flagging for awareness.

ZH surface '一键立即购买' is a semantic translation of the Amazon trademark '1-Click'. The registered trademark name in international contexts is '1-Click' (untranslated). For exam purposes this is an example term, so semantic translation is acceptable, but flagging that it is a proper trademark, not a generic concept.

EN: 'Because frequent changes of the company holding the trademark right would be disadvantageous to consumers, renewal allows...' ZH: '由于持有商标权的公司频繁变更会给消费者带来不利'. The rationale given (frequent company changes harming consumers) is pedagogically unusual. Trademark renewal exists because marks have commercial value worth protecting continuously, not specifically because 'companies change frequently'. JP original says the same; the pedagogical framing appears in the source material, so this is a source-fidelity issue rather than a translation error. Marking WARN to flag potential exam-answer confusion (examinees may repeat this framing).

Right of Publicity (パブリシティ権) is recognized in Japan by Supreme Court precedent (最高裁平成24年2月2日判決), not by a dedicated statutory provision. None of the three language definitions note the case-law basis. For IT Passport exam purposes this level of detail is not required, but the definition implies it is a general statutory right, which could mislead.

findings.term_jp: オープンソースソフトウェア (table row ZH)
findings.detail: ZH table row for OSS contains ASCII comma ',' instead of Chinese fullwidth comma '，': '源代码已公开,且允许改良和再分发的软件...版权并未被放弃。' Also uses 'copyright' (版权) rather than '著作权' used in the standalone term def, creating cross-entity terminology inconsistency within the same page.

fix_suggestion.zh: Use '，' throughout; standardize to '著作权' (not '版权') to match standalone term.

findings.term_jp: サイバーセキュリティ基本法
findings.detail: Definition accurate. Japan-jurisdiction law (平成26年法律第104号) correctly scoped.

findings.term_jp: サイバーセキュリティ基本法の対象者表 (bullet markers)
findings.detail: JP/ZH multi-item cells use '・' (interpunct bullet); EN cells use '-' (hyphen-minus). While both are readable, cross-language consistency is recommended for render parity. Specifically articles 6,7,8,9 responsibility cells have this pattern.

fix_suggestion.en: Replace '-' with '•' or standardize to same symbol across all langs.

findings.detail: JP definition uses polite ending '...禁止する法律です。' while all other JP definitions in this chunk use plain/dictionary form ('...権利', '...情報', '...法律'). Also 'その名のとおり' is colloquial framing inconsistent with formal definition style. ZH mirrors this with '顾名思义' (colloquial). EN 'As its name suggests' - same. All three languages carry an informal register into the definition.

fix_suggestion.jp: 不正なアクセスを禁止する法律
fix_suggestion.en: A law that prohibits unauthorized access to computer systems.

findings.detail: Access (アクセス) defined only as 'Connecting to a computer' / '连接到计算机' / 'Connecting to a computer.' In the context of 不正アクセス禁止法, access encompasses connecting to AND using resources of a computer system without authorization. 'Connecting' alone misses the usage dimension that is legally relevant for the Act (不正アクセス行為 = 識別符号を入力してアクセス制御機能を作動させること等).

fix_suggestion.jp: コンピュータに接続し、そのデータや機能を利用すること
fix_suggestion.zh: 连接到计算机并使用其数据或功能
fix_suggestion.en: Connecting to and using data or functions of a computer system

findings.term_jp: 管理者によるアクセス
findings.detail: Legal nuance correctly preserved: admin access itself is lawful even if subsequent acts are not.

findings.term_jp: セキュリティに関するその他の用語 (caption)
findings.detail: Table caption EN: 'Other security-related terms' uses lowercase. Compare p078 caption: 'Subjects and Their Responsibilities...' and p074 caption: 'Types of Industrial Property Rights' - both use title/sentence case consistently. Should be 'Other Security-Related Terms'.
Standalone EN def: 'A law that regulates spam email.' Table row EN: 'A law for regulating spam email'. Semantically equivalent; minor MT variation. No action needed.

EN definition 'Law Prohibiting Unauthorized Access' is the law name translated, not a definition. JP '不正なアクセスを禁止する法律' and ZH '禁止非法访问的法律' are also one-phrase labels. On a summary/review page this may be intentional compression, but it provides no pedagogical value beyond the surface term itself. Flagging for content review.

findings.term_jp: セキュリティホールからのアクセス
findings.detail: Definition accurate. 'Security hole' used as EN term; 'vulnerability' also acceptable but consistent here.
Summary def consistent with p080::2. No issues.
